era: 2013-present. Seattle. Indie Rock. Layered guitars. Big hooks. Sub Pop. E=5. Jessica Dobson -- former touring guitarist for Beck, The Shins, Conor Oberst, Yeah Yeah Yeahs. The sideplayer becomes the frontperson.

location.formative: Jessica Dobson's touring background: Beck (experimental pop), The Shins (melodic indie), Conor Oberst (folk-rock emotional directness), Yeah Yeah Yeahs (angular art-rock). Each gig an education. Multiple vocabularies absorbed.
location.career: Deep Sea Diver -- indie rock band on Sub Pop. 2013-present. Led by Jessica Dobson (vocals, guitar). Layered guitar architecture, big hooks, emotional depth.

associated_musicians.name: Beck
associated_musicians.note: Experimental pop, genre fluidity. Education in sonic experimentation and production range.

associated_musicians.name: The Shins
associated_musicians.note: Jangly melodic indie. Education in melodic precision and pop craft.

associated_musicians.name: Conor Oberst
associated_musicians.note: Bright Eyes / solo. Emotional directness, folk-inflected rock. Education in raw emotional expression.

associated_musicians.name: Yeah Yeah Yeahs
associated_musicians.note: Angular art-rock, noise-informed. Education in angular energy and performance intensity.

associated_musicians.name: Pony Time
associated_musicians.note: Self-released duo, garage rock, raw, minimal (116, E=5) vs. Sub Pop band, indie rock, layered, dense (117, E=5). Minimalism vs. maximalism.

associated_musicians.name: Nirvana
associated_musicians.note: First Sub Pop entry. Deep Sea Diver at degree 117 continues Sub Pop tradition 116 degrees later.

associated_musicians.name: Mudhoney
associated_musicians.note: Both Sub Pop, both E=5. Mudhoney raw; Deep Sea Diver layered. The label's range.

theory_nodes.concept: The sideplayer's own voice (one hundred eighteenth acoustic element -- a musician who spent years in other people's bands synthesizes multiple vocabularies into a personal voice of layered complexity; technical fluency without showmanship; big hooks inside dense architecture; the inputs are visible but the output is unique)
examples: Deep Sea Diver (degree 117) -- Jessica Dobson's synthesis of Beck + Shins + Oberst + YYYs into layered indie rock. E=5.
Contrast with the raw transmission (degree 116, Pony Time) -- minimal, stripped, immediate. The sideplayer's own voice is layered, dense, accumulated.
Contrast with the burning elegance (degree 115, Pretty Girls Make Graves) -- angular post-punk vs. layered melodic. Both E=5.
Contrast with the hypnotic engine (degree 110, Kinski) -- repetitive layers vs. melodic layers. Trance vs. song.
theory_nodes.description: The sideplayer's own voice is the 118th acoustic element. Multiple touring vocabularies synthesized into layered, hook-driven personal expression.

ancestors: The raw transmission (degree 116) -- stripped minimal. The sideplayer's own voice is the opposite: accumulated, layered, dense.
The burning elegance (degree 115) -- constructed. The sideplayer's voice is also constructed, but with different blueprints.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred eighteen acoustic elements across one hundred seventeen degrees. Track element 119 at degree 118.

theory_nodes.concept: The sideplayer-to-frontperson trajectory (one hundred eighteenth artist-city pattern -- a musician spends years in supporting roles across multiple prominent bands, then builds their own project that synthesizes all experiences; learn in background, step to front; compounded influence; inverts the usual band narrative)
examples: Jessica Dobson: touring guitarist for Beck, The Shins, Conor Oberst, Yeah Yeah Yeahs. Then frontwoman of Deep Sea Diver on Sub Pop.
Each touring gig was an education. The synthesis is greater than the sum.
Contrast with most bands: start own project first, then collaborate. Dobson did it backwards.
theory_nodes.description: The sideplayer-to-frontperson trajectory is the 118th artist-city pattern. Learn in the background, then step to the front.

ancestors: The post-streak reset (degree 116). The sideplayer trajectory is a different kind of reset: personal, not engine-wide.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred eighteen artist-city patterns across one hundred seventeen degrees. Track pattern 119 at degree 118.

theory_nodes.concept: Sub Pop recurrence at degree 117 -- the label returns after the self-released reset; label trajectory: Matador (115) to Self-Released (116) to Sub Pop (117); Seattle's flagship independent label; homecoming; the label that launched the engine (Nirvana, degree 1)
examples: Sub Pop at degree 117 (Deep Sea Diver). Also: degree 1 (Nirvana), degree 28 (Mudhoney), and others.
Label trajectory: Matador (115) -> Self-Released (116) -> Sub Pop (117). Pendulum swings.
Sub Pop homecoming: after the DIY absolute, Seattle's own label returns.
theory_nodes.description: Sub Pop returns at degree 117. After the self-released reset. Seattle's flagship label. Homecoming.

ancestors: Self-Released (degree 116). Matador (degree 115). Sub Pop swings back.
genealogy.relationship: Sub Pop recurrence. Track next appearance.

theory_nodes.concept: Layered guitar architecture -- multiple guitar tracks creating dense textural webs; effects used architecturally not decoratively; overdrive and clean coexist; hook-driven pop structure inside elaborate sonic construction; Dobson's dual role as guitarist and vocalist gives internal coherence
examples: Deep Sea Diver (degree 117) -- multiple guitar tracks, textural density, effects as structure.
Contrast with Pony Time (degree 116) -- one guitar, raw, minimal. The opposite of layered architecture.
Contrast with Minus the Bear (degree 113) -- intricate math-rock vs. layered melodic. Different guitar complexities.
theory_nodes.description: Layered guitar architecture at degree 117. Dense textural webs. Effects as structure. Hooks inside complexity.

ancestors: Minus the Bear (degree 113) -- intricate guitar. Kinski (degree 110) -- layered guitar. Different layering strategies.

cross_references.relationship: CONSECUTIVE CONTRAST. Self-released duo, garage rock, raw, minimal (116, E=5) vs. Sub Pop band, indie rock, layered, dense (117, E=5). Minimalism vs. maximalism. DIY absolute vs. Sub Pop homecoming.
GUITAR COMPLEXITY. Minus the Bear: intricate math-rock guitar (E=4). Deep Sea Diver: layered melodic guitar (E=5). Both feature guitar as sonic identity.
CONSTRUCTED DENSITY. PGMG: angular post-punk architecture (Matador, E=5). Deep Sea Diver: layered melodic architecture (Sub Pop, E=5). Different blueprints, shared construction.
SUB POP CONTINUITY. Both Sub Pop, both E=5. Mudhoney: raw garage. Deep Sea Diver: layered indie rock. The label's range.
SUB POP LINEAGE. Nirvana: first Sub Pop entry (degree 1). Deep Sea Diver: Sub Pop at degree 117. The label that launched the engine. Explosive vs. architectural.
